item.title: US Natural Gas Futures Extend Gains on Lower Production, LNG Dip
item.description: US natural gas futures rose, adding to Thursday’s 12-cent jump, as producers rein in output and feedgas to the Cameron LNG terminal in Louisiana remains lower.

item.title: Tether’s Gold Hoard Nears $20 Billion as Buying Spree Continues
item.description: Tether Holdings SA bought more than six tons of gold for its reserves in the first three months of the year, extending a buying streak that’s made it the largest known holder of bullion in the world outside of banks and nation states.

item.title: ECB’s Makhlouf Says Upside Risks to Inflation Have Intensified
item.description: European Central Bank Governing Council member Gabriel Makhlouf said yesterday’s initial assessment of the euro-area economy’s health, combined with weaker consumer and business confidence, suggests “near-term headwinds” to growth.

item.title: Exxon CEO Says Price Caps, Export Bans Will Worsen Energy Woes
item.description: Exxon Mobil Corp. Chief Executive Officer Darren Woods warned that national bans on exporting fuel hurt the global market and restrict needed supplies.

item.title: Bank of England’s Sole Dissenter Makes Case for Quick Rate Hikes
item.description: The Bank of England should be raising interest rates with greater urgency after entering the Iran war with slowing progress in its battle to bring down inflation, according to its chief economist.

item.title: Trump Family Crypto Project Quietly Sold as Holders Got Stuck
item.description: The pitch was straightforward: Invest in the cryptocurrency venture of Donald Trump and his family, back the industry’s most powerful ally at the peak of their influence, and share the spoils.

item.title: Oil Moves Lower After Report of Iranian Response to US Proposals
item.description: Oil prices moved lower, paring weekly gains in London and New York, after Axios reported that Iran replied to US amendments on a potential peace agreement, without providing details.

item.title: SoftBank-Backed Opay Hires Citi, Deutsche, JPMorgan for US IPO
item.description: Opay Digital Services Ltd. is working with Citigroup Inc., Deutsche Bank AG, and JPMorgan Chase & Co. as the Nigeria-focused payments platform prepares for an initial public offering, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Goldman Traders Brace for ‘Froth’ Removal as Stocks Get Crowded
item.description: After a record-smashing advance in April, US equity bulls are facing a bit of a reckoning: There aren’t many investors left to buy.

item.title: Two Traders Extradited to Denmark in Latest Cum-Ex Crackdown
item.description: Two traders have been extradited to Denmark to stand trial in the latest crackdown on a Cum-Ex scheme that cost the state billions of kroner.

item.title: US Stock Futures Mixed After Record as Earnings Week Wraps Up
item.description: US stock futures were little changed Friday after a record-breaking session, as investors continued to evaluate corporate results into the end of earnings season’s busiest week.

item.title: Fed’s Kashkari and Hammack Say War Has Changed Rate Outlook
item.description: Two Federal Reserve officials said they dissented over this week’s policy statement because it was no longer appropriate to signal the Fed’s next move was still likely to be an interest-rate cut.

item.title: Clear Street Signs Up to Kalshi in Prediction Markets Push
item.description: Clear Street, a broker to hedge funds and sophisticated traders, is partnering with Kalshi Inc. to give its clients access to event bets, as the fast-growing prediction markets industry seeks to increase its appeal to Wall Street.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for May 1, 2026
item.description: US equity-index futures are mixed after Thursday’s rally to a fresh record. Contracts on the S&P 500 Index are up 0.1% at 7:45 a.m. in New York.

item.title: Chevron CEO Warns Global Energy System Under ‘Extreme Stress’
item.description: Chevron Corp. is worried that global oil supplies are running dry as the US-Israel war with Iran enters its third month.

item.title: UAE’s OPEC Exit Exposes US Shale to Major New Rival
item.description: Abu Dhabi’s move means another large, low-cost operator is ready to vie for market share.

item.title: Shopify Analyst Who Saw Selloff Coming Says It’s Too Soon to Buy
item.description: The analyst who slapped the only sell rating on Shopify Inc. over valuation concerns ahead of its blistering rout said it’s still too soon to jump back in.

item.title: ‘Golden’ or ‘Garbage’: Milken Signals New Era for Private Credit
item.description: Months before Jamie Dimon’s infamous remark about more than one “cockroach” lurking in credit markets, TCW Group Inc. Chief Executive Officer Katie Koch warned the direct lending industry was due for a reckoning.

item.title: Nvidia, Microsoft, AWS Expanding Classified Military AI Use
item.description: The Pentagon has struck agreements with four more technology companies for expansive use of advanced artificial intelligence tools on classified military networks, according to a Defense Department statement and two defense officials briefed on the matter.

item.title: Meta’s AI Spending Overshadows Addiction Lawsuits, Youth Bans
item.description: Social media companies face backlash over safety of youth on their platforms

item.title: War Forces G-7 Officials to Pencil in Possible Rate Hikes
item.description: If Warsh pushes for a US rate cut, it’s likely to go against the grain of G-7 action as soon as June.

item.title: American Farmers Shun USDA Surveys as Trust in Data Erodes
item.description: American farmers are ignoring US Department of Agriculture surveys in the latest sign of distrust in government data.

item.title: Bond Traders Brace for New Gilts Selloff as Local Elections Loom
item.description: UK bond investors are shifting their focus from monetary policy to politics, with local elections next week seen as a potential catalyst for turmoil in government that could trigger a renewed selloff.

item.title: Nebius Agrees to Buy Startup That Makes AI Run Faster, Cheaper
item.description: The cloud provider’s deal for Eigen AI will help the inference business

item.title: Exxon Profit Surprises Analysts Despite Iran War’s Tumult
item.description: Exxon Mobil Corp. outperformed expectations after oil-production increases from Guyana and the Permian Basin helped offset supply losses due to the Middle East war.

item.title: Japan Can Boost the Yen But That’s Only a Short-Term Fix
item.description: Analysts doubt that intervention can do more than briefly stabilize markets.

item.title: Wall Street Is Bullish on Oracle, But Investors Aren’t Buying It
item.description: To Wall Street, Oracle Corp. is a screaming buy. To many investors, however, the company’s relationship with OpenAI, its sizable debt load and the durability of its software business have made owning the stock a risk.

item.title: Bolivia apuesta por el turismo para reactivar su economía y atraer divisas
item.description: Conocida desde hace tiempo por la minería y los combustibles fósiles, esta nación andina apuesta ahora por sus recursos naturales como forma de atraer a visitantes extranjeros —y divisas fuertes—.

item.title: Chevron Profit Beats Estimates on War-Driven Surge in Oil Prices
item.description: Chevron Corp. exceeded profit expectations as higher oil and natural gas prices, as well as supplies from the acquisition of Hess Corp., outweighed production outages from the Iran war.

item.title: Bolivia Seeks a $3 Billion Tourism Boost to Revive Its Crumbling Economy
item.description: Long known for mining and fossil fuels, the Andean nation is now eyeing its natural resources as a way to attract foreign visitors — and hard currency.
